info.name: CMP Backend
info.description:
Course Management Portal — all service endpoints via gateway :3000.

Local setup:
1. Run: node scripts/seed-emulator.js  (seeds 4 test users)
2. Import CMP_Local environment and select it
3. Run '🔐 Sign In' requests to populate tokens
4. Work through numbered folders in order for the full flow

Test users (seeded by seed-emulator.js):
  superadmin@cmp.com  SuperAdmin@123
  admin@cmp.com       Admin@12345
  student1@cmp.com    Student1@123   (pending_approval)
  student2@cmp.com    Student2@123   (pre-approved)

V2 notes:
  - Cell Service tests use adminToken for leader-role operations (admin inherits all leader permissions)
  - Analytics requires g12+ or leader+; use adminToken
  - Role Requests: student2 requests the 'student' role from admin

item.name: 🔐 Sign In (Get Tokens)
item.description: Hits the Firebase Auth emulator REST API. Tokens are auto-saved to environment variables.

item.name: 1ï¸âƒ£ Auth Service
item.description: Register, password reset (2-step OTP), logout, federated OAuth (V2).

item.name: Password Reset — Step 2: Verify OTP (expect 200 or 400)
item.description: Check Firestore passwordResetOtps collection for the OTP generated in Step 1 (emulator UI: http://localhost:4000).

item.name: Federated Sign-In — Google (V2)
item.description: Exchange a Google OAuth token for a Firebase ID token. In emulator, use a test token. Provider: 'google' or 'apple'.

exec: body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: 'student1@cmp.com', password: 'Student1@123', returnSecureToken: true }) }
if (t) { pm.environment.set('studentToken', t); pm.environment.set('idToken', t); console.log('studentToken refreshed after logout'); }

item.name: 2ï¸âƒ£ User Service — Me
item.description: Works for any signed-in role. GET /me returns user object directly (not wrapped in data:{}).

exec: if (j.uid) { pm.environment.set('userId', j.uid); console.log('userId saved:', j.uid); }
if (j.uid) { pm.environment.set('student2Id', j.uid); console.log('student2Id saved:', j.uid); }

pm.sendRequest({ url: base + '/accounts:signInWithPassword?key=' + key, method: 'POST', header: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: 'student2@cmp.com', password: 'Student2@NewPass!', returnSecureToken: true }) } }, function(err, res) {
if (!err) { const t = res.json().idToken; if (t) { pm.environment.set('student2Token', t); console.log('student2Token refreshed'); } }

item.name: Upload Avatar (student2) — multipart image
item.description: POST multipart/form-data with field 'photo'. JPEG or PNG only, max 2 MB.

item.name: Link OAuth Provider (student2)
item.description: Links a Google or Apple provider to an existing email account. Provide the OAuth token from the provider.

item.name: Unlink OAuth Provider (student2)
item.description: Unlinks google.com or apple.com from the account. Replace :provider in the URL.

item.name: 3ï¸âƒ£ User Service — Admin Manage Users
item.description: Requires admin token

item.name: Assign Role to User (V2) — e.g. grant 'student' role
item.description: Directly assigns a role to a user, bypassing the role-request flow. Body: { role: 'student' | 'leader' | 'g12' | 'admin' }

item.name: 4ï¸âƒ£ User Service — Super Admin Manage Admins
item.description: Requires super_admin token

item.name: Promote Student to Admin — saves promotedAdminId
5ï¸âƒ£ Course Service — Build a Course
item.description: Admin builds: Course → Semester → Subject Ã— 2 → Lessons, then Publishes.

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('courseId', j.id); console.log('courseId saved:', j.id); }
if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('semesterId', j.id); console.log('semesterId saved:', j.id); }
if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('subjectId', j.id); console.log('subjectId saved:', j.id); }
if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('subjectId2', j.id); console.log('subjectId2 saved:', j.id); }

item.name: Create Lesson for Subject 1 — saves lessonId
item.description: youtubeVideoId accepts full YouTube URLs. The 11-char ID is extracted and stored.

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('lessonId', j.id); console.log('lessonId saved:', j.id); }

item.name: 6ï¸âƒ£ Enrollment — student1 Registration Approval Flow
item.description: student1 is pending_approval. Admin approves the registration first, then student1 can enroll in courses.

item.name: student1 — Enroll in Course — saves enrollmentId

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('enrollmentId', j.id); console.log('enrollmentId saved:', j.id); }

item.name: Admin — Reject student1 Enrollment (expect 409 — already approved)
student2 — Enroll in Course — saves enrollmentId2

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('enrollmentId2', j.id); console.log('enrollmentId2 saved:', j.id); }

item.name: V2 Enrollment Paths (clean aliases)
item.description: V2 paths without /admin/ prefix — same logic as admin/enrollments/* but cleaner REST paths

item.name: student2 — Mark Subject 1 Complete AGAIN (idempotent — expect same record)
Admin — All Students Progress for Course
9ï¸âƒ£ Storage Service
item.description: Upload attachments (PDF/DOC/DOCX, 25 MB) and subject images (PNG/JPEG, 10 MB).

item.name: Upload PDF Attachment to Subject 1 — saves attachmentId

formdata.description: Select a .jpg or .png file to trigger 415

item.name: Upload Subject Image (PNG/JPEG, max 10 MB) — saves imageAttachmentId
item.description: POST multipart/form-data with field 'image'. PNG or JPEG only, max 10 MB.

item.name: student2 — List My Notifications — saves notificationId
📋 Audit Log
item.description: Admin+ only. Append-only audit_log. Per-user timeline requires admin+ token.

item.name: List Audit Log filtered by actor (super_admin)
Get User Audit Timeline (admin) — V2
item.description: Returns all audit events where actorUid = :uid. Accessible to admin and super_admin.

item.name: 🗔 V2 — Batches
item.description:
Batches group enrollments by intake cohort under a course. State: DRAFT → OPEN → CLOSED.
Use adminToken for all batch operations.

item.name: Create Batch (auto-opens if scheduledOpenAt is in the past) — saves batchId
item.description: scheduledOpenAt in the past → status = OPEN immediately. In the future → status = DRAFT.

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('batchId', j.id); console.log('batchId saved:', j.id); }

item.name: Update Batch (name only — date changes blocked on non-DRAFT)
Create DRAFT Batch (future scheduledOpenAt) — saves draftBatchId

exec: if (j.id) { pm.environment.set('draftBatchId', j.id); console.log('draftBatchId saved:', j.id); }

item.name: âš¡ Course Lifecycle — Archive, Restore & Delete
item.description: Complete the course lifecycle. Restore transitions ARCHIVED → DRAFT so it can be re-published.

item.name: Restore Course — ARCHIVED → DRAFT (V2)
item.description: Transitions an archived course back to DRAFT. Must be re-published to become visible again.

item.name: ðŸŽ« V2 — Role Requests
item.description:
Members request non-member roles (student, leader, g12). Admin approves or rejects.
Flow: student2 (member) requests 'student' role → admin approves → student2 gains student access.

item.name: Create Role Request (student2 requests 'student' role) — saves roleRequestId
Admin — Get Role Request by ID
Admin — Approve Role Request (grants role on user doc atomically)
ðŸ˜ V2 — Cell Service
item.description:
Cell groups, member management, join requests, cell reports.
Note: adminToken is used for leader-role operations (admin inherits all leader+ permissions).
For join requests, member/student2 token is used.
Cell reports use X-Idempotency-Key for safe retries.

item.name: Create Cell Group (admin as leader) — saves cellId
Add Members to Cell (batch add)
item.description: Body: { userUids: string[] }. Idempotent — skips UIDs already in cell.

item.name: Create Join Request (student2 → cell) — saves joinRequestId
Admin — Reject Join Request (create another request first)
File Cell Report (idempotent via X-Idempotency-Key) — saves cellReportId
item.description: Use the same X-Idempotency-Key to test idempotency — second call returns the same record.

item.name: File Cell Report again (same idempotency key — expect same record)
📊 V2 — Analytics Service
item.description:
Read-only dashboards from pre-aggregated analytics_snapshots written by scheduled-jobs.
Requires leader+ for weekly/attendance/meeting-types; g12+ for growth/participation/export.
Use adminToken (admin inherits all permissions).

item.name: Export Attendance CSV (g12+)
item.description: Replace :chart with: attendance | meeting-types | growth | participation | cells/weekly

item.name: ðŸ¥ Health Checks
item.description:
Hit each service directly (bypassing the gateway) to confirm all are alive.
/healthz = liveness probe
/readyz  = readiness probe (Firestore connection check)
